title: Agents That Know Too Much: A Data-Centric Survey of Privacy in LLM Agents
abstract: arXiv:2606.26627v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Large language model agents increasingly query databases, search document collections, call external APIs, remember past interactions, and act on a user's behalf. As they move from answering questions to operating over sensitive data, privacy becomes harder to enforce. An agent touches many data sources, runs multi-step workflows, keeps state across sessions, and acts with delegated permissions. Sensitive information can therefore leak not only through its final answer but through the queries it issues, the intermediate results it handles, the memory it writes, and the messages it exchanges with other agents. We survey the privacy of LLM agents from a data-centric view, organizing the field around the data an agent touches rather than by attack type, and we use data agent as shorthand for an LLM agent that works with data. Research on these risks is active but scattered across retrieval-augmented generation, text-to-SQL interfaces, age

Title: Agents That Know Too Much: A Data-Centric Survey of Privacy in LLM Agents
Abstract: arXiv:2606.26627v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Large language model agents increasingly query databases, search document collections, call external APIs, remember past interactions, and act on a user's behalf. As they move from answering questions to operating over sensitive data, privacy becomes harder to enforce. An agent touches many data sources, runs multi-step workflows, keeps state across sessions, and acts with delegated permissions. Sensitive information can therefore leak not only through its final answer but through the queries it issues, the intermediate results it handles, the memory it writes, and the messages it exchanges with other agents. We survey the privacy of LLM agents from a data-centric view, organizing the field around the data an agent touches rather than by attack type, and we use data agent as shorthand for an LLM agent that works with data. translated_title: 知道太多的人工智能体：LLM代理中隐私问题的数据中心化调查
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.26627v1 公告类型：交叉 摘要：大型语言模型代理日益频繁地查询数据库、搜索文档集合、调用外部API、记住过去的交互，并代表用户执行操作。随着它们从回答问题转向处理敏感数据，隐私保护变得更加困难。一个代理接触众多数据源，执行多步骤工作流，跨会话保持状态，并以委托权限行事。因此，敏感信息不仅可能通过其最终答案泄露，还可能通过其发出的查询、处理的中间结果、写入的记忆以及与其他代理交换的消息泄露。我们从以数据为中心的角度调查LLM代理的隐私问题，围绕代理接触的数据而非攻击类型来组织这一领域，并使用“数据代理”作为处理数据的LLM代理的简称。关于这些风险的研究虽然活跃，但分散在检索增强生成、文本到SQL接口等领域。
tldr: 本文从数据为中心的角度系统调查了LLM代理在处理敏感数据时面临的隐私泄露风险，指出隐私泄露不仅存在于最终输出，还涉及查询、中间结果、记忆和代理间通信等多个环节。
en_tdlr: This paper systematically surveys privacy leakage risks in LLM agents handling sensitive data from a data-centric perspective, highlighting that leaks occur not only in final outputs but also through queries, intermediate results, memory, and inter-agent communications.
